name description
Fever High body temperature often associated with infection
Cough A sudden and often repetitively occurring protective reflex
Headache Pain in any region of the head
Fatigue A subjective feeling of tiredness that is distinct from sleepiness
Nausea A sensation of unease and discomfort in the upper stomach
Sore Throat Pain or irritation in the throat that can occur with swallowing
Shortness of Breath An intense tightening in the chest or feeling of suffocation
Chills Feelings of coldness accompanied by shivering
Muscle Pain Discomfort or pain in the muscles throughout the body
Loss of Taste Inability to perceive basic flavors normally
Amlapitta Acid-peptic digestive disorder
Sandhivata Degenerative joint disorder
Anidra Sleep disturbance
Kasa, Vataja Dry cough with Vata predominance
Arsha Hemorrhoidal disease
Fever (Jwara) Traditional Ayurvedic term for elevated body temperature.
Cough (Kasa) Ayurvedic classification for respiratory irritation.
Indigestion (Ajirna) Functional digestive disturbance according to Ayush.
Headache (Shiroroga) General term for cephalalgia in Ayurvedic texts.
Cold (Pratishyaya) Common cold symptoms including nasal congestion.

Jwara Fever with chills and body ache

Atisara Diarrhea with loose stools
Chardi Nausea and vomiting
Hrdroga Heart disease or chest discomfort
Shwasa Breathlessness or dyspnea

Cough Dry or productive cough
Fever Elevated body temperature
Diarrhea Loose or watery stools
Nausea Feeling of sickness before vomiting
Dyspnea Difficulty breathing or breathlessness
Edema Swelling due to fluid accumulation
Abdominal pain Pain or cramps in the abdomen
Tachycardia Rapid heart rate above normal
Anorexia Loss of appetite
Polydipsia Excessive thirst beyond normal
